description: A rule means 'surface this notification'. In v1 all rules deliver: matching any rule = deliver, no match = discard. No DISCARD (exception) action exists in v1.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Stable UUID identifier assigned at creation.

fields.name: source_app
fields.description: Matched against the notification's source_app field. See matching_semantics.

fields.name: source_account
fields.description: Matched against the notification's source_account field. See matching_semantics.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Matched against the notification's title field. See matching_semantics.

matching_semantics.note: Applies to source_app, source_account, and title fields.

wildcard.meaning: Matches any value for this field.

pattern.meaning: Field must match this glob pattern. * = any sequence of characters.

exact.meaning: Field must equal this value exactly.

coverage_relation.description: Rule R covers rule S if every notification matched by S is also matched by R, field-by-field.
rules: * in R covers any value in S (including other patterns).
A pattern in R covers an exact value in S if the value matches the pattern.
An exact value in R covers only the same exact value in S.

http_api.create: POST /rules — 201 with {id}. 400 if no fields are set.
http_api.list: GET /rules — 200 with array of rules for the authenticated user.

invariants: In v1, all rules deliver. Multiple matching rules cannot conflict.
Specificity resolution is reserved for v2 when DISCARD exception rules are introduced.
INV-5: A rule change takes effect for all future notifications immediately; it does not retroactively affect already-routed notifications.
INV-7: The rule store never contains two rules in a subset-or-superset relationship. Creating a rule automatically removes any existing rule that is a superset or subset of the new rule.
